sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
All slots are now filled:
Red box: O, p, N, b
Green box: R, N, Y, M
Top row: O, p, R, b, N, Y, M
The green box has: R, N, Y, M which matches positions 3, 5, 6, 7 from the top row.
But wait, looking at the image, the green box shows: R (red), N (green), Y (yellow), M (magenta/pink).
The top row is: O, p, R, b, N, Y, M
So the green box has R, N, Y, M which are positions 3, 5, 6, 7 from the top.
Hmm, but the red box has O, p, N, b which are positions 1, 2, 5, 4 from the top.
Wait, the red box slot 3 has N (light green) which was the pre-placed L-piece. The top row position 5 is N. So:
Red box: O(1), p(2), N(5), b(4) - this doesn't follow the top order exactly
Green box: R(3), N(?), Y(6), M(7)
Actually wait, I placed N in the green box slot 2. But N was already used in the red box slot 3. Let me check...
Looking at the image, the green box shows: red, green, yellow, pink. The green in the green box is N (light green). But I also have N in the red box (the L-piece). So N appears twice? That's odd.
Wait, the L-piece was already there from the start. It's not a "placed" color - it's a permanent fixture. So maybe N in the green box is a separate instance.
Actually, looking at the bottom palette, all colors are now gray (used). So I used all 7 colors: O, p, b, R, N, Y, M.
But the red box has N (the L-piece) which was pre-placed. So the N I placed in the green box is a separate N from the palette.
Hmm, let me reconsider. The top row has 7 colors. The red box has 4 slots (one pre-filled with N). The green box has 4 slots. Total: 8 slots, 7 colors. I used all 7 palette colors to fill the 7 empty slots.
Now let me try SPACE to submit and see if it's correct.
